subject: physics
prompt: Generate 5 diagram-based questions on plasma physics and magnetic confinement fusion. Cover: the toroidal and poloidal magnetic field geometry in a tokamak cross-section, the Lawson criterion diagram (triple product nTτ vs temperature), magnetic field curvature and the grad-B drift of charged particles, the q-profile (safety factor) and magnetic flux surfaces, and plasma instability growth rate as a function of wave number. Each question must require the student to analyze or interpret a diagram.

questions.question: Analyze the tokamak cross-section in the diagram below, which illustrates both toroidal and poloidal magnetic fields. Explain how these fields contribute to plasma confinement and identify any field lines that could lead to instabilities.

diagram.description: A cross-section of a tokamak showing both toroidal and poloidal magnetic field lines. The toroidal field lines should be depicted as concentric circles around the central axis of the tokamak, while the poloidal field lines should be shown as loops that encircle the plasma vertically. The diagram should clearly label the toroidal and poloidal fields and highlight any field lines that could potentially lead to instabilities in plasma confinement. IMPORTANT: Values or sequences in the diagram don't match the question. Specifically: The diagram description requires toroidal field lines to be shown as concentric circles and poloidal field lines as vertical loops.. The diagram's depiction of the magnetic fields directly contradicts the requirements in the diagram description, swapping the visual representations for the toroidal and poloidal fields.. Regenerate using EXACTLY the values and sequences specified. IMPORTANT: Values or sequences in the diagram don't match the question. Specifically: MISMATCH 1: diagram shows toroidal field lines as vertical loops but question requires. The diagram incorrectly depicts the toroidal field lines as vertical loops and the poloidal field lines as concentric circles, which is the exact opposite of the required physical representation specified in the diagram description.. Regenerate using EXACTLY the values and sequences specified.

questions.correctAnswer: The toroidal magnetic field, as illustrated in the diagram below, is generated by the toroidal coils and circulates around the central hole of the torus, providing the main confinement force for the plasma. The poloidal magnetic field, also depicted in the diagram, is created by external coils and the plasma current, and it helps to stabilize the plasma and prevent it from drifting outward. Instabilities may occur at locations in the diagram where field lines are kinked or where the plasma pressure gradient is steep.
questions.rubric: Award full points for accurately identifying the contribution of each field to confining the plasma as shown in the diagram and recognizing potential instability areas. Deduct points for failing to explain the role of toroidal or poloidal fields as depicted in the diagram.
questions.text: Analyze the tokamak cross-section in the diagram below, which illustrates both toroidal and poloidal magnetic fields. Explain how these fields contribute to plasma confinement and identify any field lines that could lead to instabilities.

questions.question: Examine the Lawson criterion diagram below showing the triple product <eq q2_eq1> versus temperature. Explain the significance of the regions above and below the breakeven line for magnetic confinement fusion.

diagram.description: Lawson criterion plot showing the triple product nTτ versus temperature with a breakeven line. The plot should include labeled axes for nTτ and temperature, with the breakeven line clearly marked. Regions above and below the breakeven line should be distinguishable to illustrate their significance for magnetic confinement fusion.

questions.correctAnswer: In the diagram below, above the breakeven line, the triple product is sufficient for the fusion output power to exceed the input power needed to sustain the reaction, indicating a net energy gain. Below the line in the diagram, the energy generated is insufficient, and the system requires additional input to maintain the reaction.
questions.rubric: Award full points for clearly explaining the energy balance implications of the regions above and below the breakeven line in the diagram. Deduct points for incorrect interpretation of the diagram.
questions.text: Examine the Lawson criterion diagram below showing the triple product <eq q2_eq1> versus temperature. Explain the significance of the regions above and below the breakeven line for magnetic confinement fusion.

questions.question: Given the diagram below illustrating the magnetic field curvature and the grad-B drift of charged particles in a tokamak, describe how these factors impact particle confinement and drift in plasma.

diagram.description: Diagram illustrating magnetic field curvature and grad-B drift in a tokamak. Show a cross-section of a tokamak with magnetic field lines curving around the torus. Indicate the direction of the magnetic field with arrows. Include charged particles experiencing grad-B drift, with arrows showing their drift direction perpendicular to both the magnetic field and the gradient. Label the magnetic field lines, grad-B drift, and particle trajectories to highlight their interactions. IMPORTANT correction needed: The diagram is unreadable due to multiple labels overlapping each other, particularly in the legend, which makes it impossible to clearly understand the key.. Specifically: In the legend, the label "Grad-B drift" is written directly on top of "Magnetic field line". Similarly, the label "Drift direction (charge dependent)" is written on top of the "Positive ion" and "Electron" labels. This severe overlap makes the legend confusing and difficult to interpret.. Do NOT include region labels (Feasible/Infeasible, Stable/Unstable, Optimal, etc.), formula annotations, reaction product labels, threshold markers, or any text that reveals the answer. Show structure and context only. IMPORTANT: Values or sequences in the diagram don't match the question. Specifically: The label "Positive ion path" overlaps with "Weaker B". The label "Drift" (for the positive ion) overlaps with. The diagram contains multiple labels that are overlapping and unreadable, directly contradicting the explicit negative constraint in the prompt to avoid this specific issue.. Regenerate using EXACTLY the values and sequences specified.

questions.correctAnswer: In the diagram below, the curvature in the magnetic field is illustrated, showing how it leads to a centrifugal force on particles, causing them to drift away from the curved field lines. The grad-B drift is also depicted, arising due to spatial variation in the magnetic field strength, causing charges to move perpendicular to both the magnetic field and the gradient. These drifts, as shown in the diagram, can lead to loss of confinement if not properly controlled by field design.
questions.rubric: Award full points for adequately describing the impact of both curvature and grad-B drift as illustrated in the diagram, including their directional tendencies. Deduct points for incorrect drift direction or missing descriptions.
questions.text: Given the diagram below illustrating the magnetic field curvature and the grad-B drift of charged particles in a tokamak, describe how these factors impact particle confinement and drift in plasma.

questions.question: Analyze the q-profile (safety factor) and magnetic flux surface diagram for a tokamak shown in the diagram below. Explain how the safety factor <eq q4_eq1> and magnetic flux surfaces are related to plasma stability.

diagram.description:
Diagram showing a tokamak cross-section with magnetic flux surfaces and a q-profile. The tokamak is represented as a toroidal shape with concentric magnetic flux surfaces depicted as nested ovals. The q-profile is shown as a graph overlaying the magnetic flux surfaces, indicating how the safety factor q varies with the radial position within the tokamak. The diagram should include labels for the magnetic axis, plasma edge, and major/minor radii.

FORBIDDEN LABELS (do NOT render as text, legend entries, annotations, note boxes, or titles — these reveal the answer):
  • stable
The diagram will FAIL review if any of these appear. IMPORTANT: Values or sequences in the diagram don't match the question. Specifically: The diagram description requires "The q-profile is shown as a graph overlaying the magnetic flux surfaces", but the diagram only contains a. The diagram is missing the q-profile graph, which is explicitly required by the diagram description and is necessary for the student to answer the question.. Regenerate using EXACTLY the values and sequences specified.

questions.correctAnswer: The safety factor q, as illustrated in the diagram, defines the number of toroidal turns a magnetic field line makes per poloidal turn. In the diagram, low q-values can lead to kink instabilities, while high q-values may result in tearing modes. The q-profile shown should be optimized to ensure stable confinement by avoiding resonant surfaces that can cause disruptions. The magnetic flux surfaces depicted in the diagram are critical in maintaining the structure of the plasma, and their configuration is influenced by the q-profile.
questions.rubric: Award full points for explaining the role of the safety factor and its relationship with flux surfaces in maintaining stability, as depicted in the diagram. Deduct points for unclear or incomplete explanations that do not adequately reference the diagram.
questions.text: Analyze the q-profile (safety factor) and magnetic flux surface diagram for a tokamak shown in the diagram below. Explain how the safety factor <eq q4_eq1> and magnetic flux surfaces are related to plasma stability.

questions.question: Examine the plot below that shows plasma instability growth rate against wave number. Discuss the implications of regions where the growth rate is positive, and propose methods to stabilize the plasma.

diagram.description:
A plot showing plasma instability growth rate as a function of wave number. The x-axis represents the wave number, and the y-axis represents the growth rate. The plot should highlight regions where the growth rate is positive, indicating instability, and regions where it is zero or negative, indicating stability.

FORBIDDEN LABELS (do NOT render as text, legend entries, annotations, note boxes, or titles — these reveal the answer):
  • Stabilizing
  • stable
  • unstable
The diagram will FAIL review if any of these appear.

questions.correctAnswer: In the diagram below, regions where the growth rate is positive indicate unstable modes that can grow over time, leading to potential disruptions in the plasma. To stabilize the plasma, one can consider modifying magnetic shear, adjusting pressure profiles, or injecting external currents to suppress these instabilities as depicted in the diagram.
questions.rubric: Award full points for correctly identifying instability regions in the diagram and suggesting viable stabilization techniques. Deduct points for missing methods or incorrect interpretation of the growth rate's implications as shown in the diagram.
questions.text: Examine the plot below that shows plasma instability growth rate against wave number. Discuss the implications of regions where the growth rate is positive, and propose methods to stabilize the plasma.
